sections.paragraph:
μίμημα, ατος, τό (μιμέομαι; Aeschyl. et al.; Musonius 90, 40 a human being is μ. θεοῦ; Polemo, Decl. 2, 32 p. 27, 7; OGI 383, 63; 404, 26; Wsd 9:8. Oft. Philo; Jos., Bell. 7, 142, Ant. 12, 75; Just., A II, 13, 6; SibOr 8, 116) that which is imitated, copy, image δέχεσθαι τὰ μ. τῆς ἀληθοῦς ἀγάπης receive the copies of True Love (i.e. of Christ), perh. w. ref. to Ign. and fellow prisoners (s. JKleist, transl., ad loc.) Pol 1:1 (Herm. Wr. 382, 18 Sc. μ. τ. ἀληθείας).—DELG s.v. μῖμος.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Musonius Musonius = Gaius Musonius Rufus, I a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

perh. perh. = perhaps

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Ign. Ign. = Ignatius; always simply I. when cited with one of his epistles

transl. transl. = translate, translation

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 651
